mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held below 1% alongside gradual, telegraphed increases signals continued accommodative monetary policy, which supports equity valuations by keeping the discount rate on future cash flows low, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.65% as of t-1mo, well below historical tightening thresholds
regime_hint: any regime with sub-2% policy rate
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns during low-rate periods are not statistically different from full-sample average (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE falls below 5.0% and continues declining, labor market tightness raises expectations of wage-driven inflation and eventual Fed tightening, causing equity markets to price in higher future discount rates and produce negative forward 20-day S&P 500 returns once the unemployment rate crosses this threshold.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 5.0% and declines further for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.8% as of t-1mo, near cycle lows
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tightening labor market
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE is below 5.0% and falling, or Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) reflects broad-based consumer and investment demand strength that jointly signal robust nominal GDP growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter; neither series alone is sufficient because retail strength without housing follow-through often reflects transitory consumption rather than durable expansion.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 472142 million and HOUST at 1226 thousand units as of latest snapshot
failure_condition: Joint RSAFS+HOUST acceleration signal fails to outperform either single-series signal in out-of-sample Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window, or combined signal Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of sustained industrial production growth above trend, INDPRO increases signal expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and corporate earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter; this relationship strengthens specifically during expansion regimes and weakens or reverses during late-cycle overheating when rate hike fears dominate.
regime_hint: early-to-mid cycle expansion with low policy rates
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO-return relationship fails to hold (correlation flips sign) in expansion regime subsample, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window during expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth reflects near-term corporate hiring confidence and consumer income generation, and positive PAYEMS surprises produce a short-lived positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the following week as markets reprice growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 145554 thousand persons as of t-1mo
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive PAYEMS prints are not significantly different from returns following negative prints (t-test p>0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When headline CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth accelerates past a threshold implying inflation above the Fed's implicit comfort zone, markets anticipate accelerated policy tightening, raising discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20d forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when CPI YoY exceeds 2.5%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) combined with rising retail sales (RSAFS) jointly indicate that aggregate demand expansion is translating into actual consumption rather than inventory buildup, and this joint acceleration produces positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter; GDP alone is too lagged and low-frequency to be actionable without RSAFS confirmation.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16804.8 billion as of t-4mo
failure_condition: Combined GDPC1+RSAFS signal underperforms RSAFS-only signal in out-of-sample Rank IC over any 3-year window, or joint signal Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: During periods when unemployment is low (UNRATE below 5%) and industrial production is rising, the economy is in a tight-capacity regime where further INDPRO gains are read by markets as inflationary rather than growth-positive, producing negative short-horizon S&P 500 returns; the same INDPRO increase would be growth-positive in a slack-labor regime.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE below 5.0% and INDPRO rising month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.8% and INDPRO at 104.5548 as of latest snapshot
regime_hint: tight labor market / late-cycle capacity constraint
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following INDPRO increases under tight-labor conditions are not significantly more negative than under slack-labor conditions (p>0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year window.
